channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Guantanamo's prisoners have reached a pivotal moment. So has the 9/11 case. Here's what to know
item.description: After 23 years, the fate of the last remaining Guantanamo detainees swept up worldwide after al-Qaida’s shattering attacks on Sept. 11, 2001, is reaching a pivotal moment this month.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: FEMA'S Got the Bill
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska sues Biden administration for 'irrational' restrictions on Trump-era oil and gas drilling mandate
item.description: The state of Alaska is suing the Biden administration over claims that they are violating the statutory mandate of Congress by limiting the an oil and gas lease in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia government grinds to a halt as hospitals, residents hit by colossal water plant failure
item.description: Virginia's government ground to a halt, and thousands were left without water after a colossal failure at the city's treatment plant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he is still considering pre-emptive pardons for Trump targets Liz Cheney, Fauci and others
item.description: President Biden said he was still considering pre-emptive pardons for President-elect Donald Trump's political foes, including former GOP Congresswoman Liz Cheney and Dr. Anthony Fauci, during his final interview with a print publication before leaving the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS has improved taxpayer services but is slow to resolve ID theft, an independent watchdog says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS boosted taxpayer services through Democrats’ Inflation Reduction Act but still faces processing claims from a coronavirus pandemic-era tax credit program and is slow to resolve certain identity theft cases, according to an independent watchdog report released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump asks Supreme Court to block sentencing in hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Colorado governor walks a tightrope in approaching Trump. Deportations likely to be the first test
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado Gov. Jared Polis donned safety glasses and seized the handle of the electric saw, guiding the buzzing blade down through a stack of printed-out executive orders dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Virginia Democrats keep control of Statehouse in first elections after Trump win
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats preserved their Statehouse majority on Tuesday in the first test of voters’ energy since President-elect Donald Trump’s win in November, which left many party members across the country reckoning with their losses in federal elections.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: “Look, the Panama Canal is vital to our country, it’s being operated by China, China. And we gave the Panama Canal to Panama, we didn’t give it to China,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘Intention’ is to increase debt limit in massive Trump agenda bill
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said congressional Republicans plan to increase the nation’s borrowing limit as a part of a massive party-line bill they are crafting that will encapsulate President-elect Trump’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers prepare to take office
item.description: The Minnesota House will enter the 2025 legislative session with a new power structure and also a new home base down the block as its typical digs undergo construction.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: O'Malley touts Congressional Black Caucus members in DNC chair bid
item.description: “They are proven winners, and together, we will reengage, reconnect, and rebuild our party,” Former Maryland Gov. Martin O’Malley (D) said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Millions of public workers are set to get higher Social Security benefits. Here's why
item.description: Public service workers, including some teachers, firefighters and police officers, may soon see their Social Security payments increase by hundreds of dollars monthly.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, PIX 11

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden bans new offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters
item.description: President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, an effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sales of Minnesota blackout license plates exceed 250,000 in first year
item.description: More than a quarter-million vehicles in the state now have the plates featuring a simple design of white letters and numbers on a black background.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Krueger, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With Board Chair Nora Vargas ending her term, here's who's vying to replace her
item.description: As of Friday, there are four people who have shown interest in running to replace Chair Nora Vargas on the San Diego County Board of Supervisors at the end of her term.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5
